$description: Perforce Force UI — Semantic tokens, dark theme overrides. Only color tokens change between themes. Typography, spacing, radius, motion, z-index, and layout are theme-invariant and inherited from semantic-light. Applied via [data-theme='dark'] selector.

surface.$description: Primary content surface in dark mode.

muted.$description: Secondary/subtle surface in dark mode. Slightly darker than surface.

emphasis.$description: Chrome surface in dark mode (topbar, sidebar). Slightly darker than surface — mirrors the subtle light-mode contrast between surface and emphasis.

inverse.$description: Inverted surface in dark mode. White.

overlay.$description: Modal overlay scrim. Same in both themes.

interactive-hover.$description: Hover background for interactive elements in dark mode.

interactive-active.$description: Active/selected background in dark mode. Deep cyan tint.

primary.$description: Solid primary background in dark mode. Cyan is the primary brand color in dark mode (#00cfff). Pair with color.text.on-primary (black).

primary-hover.$description: Hover state for primary in dark mode. Brighter than resting cyan.500.

primary-active.$description: Pressed state for primary in dark mode. Darker than resting cyan.500.

primary-subtle.$description: Subtle primary-tinted background in dark mode. Deep cyan tint.

primary-subtle-hover.$description: Hover for subtle primary-tinted backgrounds in dark mode. One palette step lighter than primary-subtle.

on-brand.$description: Resting fill for on-brand buttons over brand backgrounds in dark mode. Semi-transparent white reads against the cyan primary surface.

on-brand-hover.$description: Hover fill for on-brand buttons over cyan primary backgrounds in dark mode.

secondary.$description: Solid secondary background in dark mode. Indigo is the secondary brand color in dark mode. Pair with color.text.on-secondary (black) — indigo.300 (#a89bff) is light enough that black passes AAA (~9:1) while white fails.

destructive.$description: Solid destructive background in dark mode.

destructive-hover.$description: Hover for destructive in dark mode.

destructive-active.$description: Pressed for destructive in dark mode.

success.$description: Solid success background in dark mode. Lighter green for contrast against dark surfaces. Pair with color.text.on-success (black).

success-hover.$description: Hover for solid success backgrounds in dark mode.

success-subtle.$description: Success subtle background in dark mode. Dark green tint.

warning.$description: Solid warning background in dark mode. Pair with color.text.on-warning (black).

warning-hover.$description: Hover for solid warning backgrounds in dark mode.

warning-subtle.$description: Warning subtle background in dark mode. Dark orange tint.

error-subtle.$description: Error subtle background in dark mode. Dark cranberry tint.

info.$description: Solid info background in dark mode. Pair with color.text.on-info (black).

info-hover.$description: Hover for solid info backgrounds in dark mode.

info-subtle.$description: Info subtle background in dark mode. Dark blue tint.

primary.$description: Primary text in dark mode. Near-white.

on-primary.$description: Text on solid primary backgrounds in dark mode. Black for contrast on cyan.500 (#00cfff) — black-on-cyan passes AAA (~12:1).

on-secondary.$description: Text on solid secondary (indigo.300) backgrounds in dark mode. Black — indigo.300 (#a89bff) is light enough that black passes AAA (~9:1) while white fails.

on-success.$description: Text on solid success backgrounds in dark mode. Black because bg.success resolves to green-300 (light) in dark mode. Use on color.bg.success.

on-warning.$description: Text on solid warning backgrounds in dark mode. Black. Use on color.bg.warning.

on-info.$description: Text on solid info backgrounds in dark mode. Black because bg.info resolves to blue-300 (light) in dark mode. Use on color.bg.info.

on-error.$description: Text on solid error/destructive (cranberry-300) backgrounds in dark mode. Black passes WCAG AA. Use on color.bg.destructive.

on-inverse.$description: Text on inverse (white) backgrounds in dark mode.

link.$description: Link text in dark mode. Cyan-300 (#99eeff) provides strong contrast on color.bg.surface. Do NOT use on color.bg.primary-hover (both resolve to light cyan, low contrast). Do NOT use on color.bg.primary — use color.text.on-primary instead.

link-hover.$description: Link hover in dark mode. Lighter than resting cyan.300.

interactive-active.$description: Active nav/selected item text in dark mode. Pair with color.bg.interactive-active.

primary-brand.$description: Brand-colored text in dark mode. Cyan-300 (#99eeff) provides strong contrast on color.bg.surface. Do NOT use on color.bg.primary-hover (both resolve to light cyan, low contrast). On solid primary backgrounds, use color.text.on-primary instead.

focus.$description: Focus ring border in dark mode. Cyan-400 for visibility against dark surfaces.

success.$description: Success accent border in dark mode.

warning.$description: Warning accent border in dark mode.

info.$description: Info accent border in dark mode.

primary.$description: Primary brand border in dark mode. Cyan-400 for outline buttons and selected/active borders.

input.$description: Input/select/textarea/button-secondary boundary. Meets WCAG 1.4.11 ≥3:1 against bg.surface. For decorative dividers use border.default.

interactive.$description: Interactive icon in dark mode. Cyan-400 for clickable standalone icons.

warning-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on dark/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.warning (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

info-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on dark/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.info (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

success-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on dark/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.success (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

error-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on dark/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.error (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

1.$description: Primary chart series in dark mode. Cyan is the primary brand color in dark mode.

2.$description: Second chart series in dark mode. Indigo is the secondary brand color in dark mode.

3.$description: Third chart series in dark mode.

4.$description: Fourth chart series in dark mode.

5.$description: Fifth chart series in dark mode.

6.$description: Sixth chart series in dark mode.

shadow.$description: Dark mode shadows use stronger opacity for visibility against dark surfaces.

focus-ring.$description: Two-stop focus ring; inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Dark mode uses cyan-400 for the outer band.

focus-ring-error.$description: Two-stop error focus ring; inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Dark mode uses cranberry-300 for the outer band.

focus-ring-on-brand.$description: Two-stop focus ring for elements sitting on bg.primary; inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Use this when an element sits on bg.primary.
